items.question_text: Under 5-6-1, which of the following is NOT a listed purpose for vectoring aircraft in controlled airspace?
items.explanation: 5-6-1a lists: separation, safety, noise abatement, operational advantage, confidence maneuver, or pilot request. 'Fuel efficiency' is not listed.

choices.text: Separation.
Fuel efficiency.
Confidence maneuver.
Noise abatement.

items.question_text: NOTE: Under 5-6-1, VFR aircraft not at an altitude assigned by ATC may be vectored at _____ altitude.
items.explanation: The note under 5-6-1c states VFR aircraft not at an assigned altitude may be vectored at ANY altitude, with the pilot responsible for CFR compliance.

choices.text: any

items.question_text: Under 5-6-2g, aircraft may not be vectored off an Obstacle Departure Procedure until at or above what altitude?
items.explanation: 5-6-2g: aircraft may not be vectored off an ODP until at or above the MVA/MIA, at which time the ODP is canceled. Once vectored off, the ODP is canceled and cannot be resumed.

choices.text: At or above the MVA/MIA.
At or above the ODP published altitude.
At or above 1,000 feet AGL.
At or above the minimum vectoring altitude published on the approach plate.

items.question_text: NOTE: Under 5-6-2d, after a climb via or descend via clearance has been issued, what effect does vectoring off a SID/STAR have on the published restrictions?
items.explanation: The note states: after a climb via/descend via clearance has been issued, a vector/deviation off the SID/STAR cancels ALL published altitude and speed restrictions on the procedure.

choices.text: It cancels ALL published altitude and speed restrictions on the procedure.
It only cancels speed restrictions; altitude restrictions remain.
It only cancels altitude restrictions; speed restrictions remain.
The restrictions remain in effect until the pilot acknowledges the vector.

items.question_text: Under 5-6-2e, in which case is the controller NOT required to inform an aircraft of its position when terminating radar navigational guidance?
items.explanation: 5-6-2e3: position information is not required when the aircraft is RNAV, FMS, or DME equipped and being vectored toward a VORTAC/TACAN or waypoint within the NAVAID service volume.

choices.text: When the aircraft is RNAV, FMS, or DME equipped and being vectored toward a VORTAC/TACAN or waypoint within the NAVAID service volume.
When the aircraft is established within the protected airspace for the nonradar route.
When the aircraft is operating VFR and on an assigned heading.
When the aircraft has already been cleared for the approach.

items.question_text: Under 5-6-3 TERMINAL, when vectoring a departing IFR aircraft or one executing a missed approach below the minimum altitude for IFR operations, what data block condition must NOT exist?
items.explanation: 5-6-3a: vectors below minimum IFR altitude may be applied when ISR is NOT displayed in the full data block, and one of the obstacle separation conditions is met.

choices.text: ISR must not be displayed in the full data block.
TRK must not be displayed in the data block.
CST must be displayed in the data block.
NAT must be displayed in the data block.

items.situation: An aircraft was issued a 'descend via' clearance on the Ksino One arrival. You now vector the aircraft off the STAR for traffic. You later instruct the aircraft to resume the procedure.
items.question_text: Under 5-6-2f, what must you include when clearing the aircraft to resume the procedure?

choices.text: Issue or reissue all applicable published crossing restrictions, or instruct the aircraft to Descend Via the STAR.
Simply say 'resume the Ksino One arrival' because the restrictions reactivate automatically.
Only issue the next waypoint crossing restriction and maintain the current altitude.
Cancel the STAR entirely and issue a completely new clearance.

items.explanation: 5-6-2F requires issuing/reissuing all applicable restrictions or instructing Climb Via/Descend Via when resuming a procedure with published crossing restrictions.
